title: Ship Rotation, Thrust, Inertia, Drag, and Speed Limits · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: A community arcade accessibility coordinator. They need a versioned event-update-render loop with remappable keyboard and controller actions, focus-loss safety, explicit vector units, frame-rate-independent ship motion, pause, and text traces that make every critical state inspectable without relying on animation. Planned artifact: Deliver Accessible Input and Ship Prototype with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through predi…
objectives: Pin Python 3.14 and released pygame-ce 2.5.7 as a mutually supported stack while distinguishing package name from import name.
Order event intake, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, presentation, and timing once per frame.
Store configurable action bindings, detect conflicts, offer alternatives, and avoid unmodified character-key shortcuts where inappropriate.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Runtime Choice
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Asteroids team handles case 5202 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to integrate rotation, thrust, inertia, drag, and speed limits with frame-rate-independent Vector2 motion; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Runtime Choice.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Runtime Choice, keep Runtime Choice connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Audit the initial evidence packet · Project Ship Feel Evidence
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Asteroids team handles case 6821 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to integrate rotation, thrust, inertia, drag, and speed limits with frame-rate-independent Vector2 motion; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Tune rotation, acceleration, drag, cap, input latency, and feedback against recorded player tasks and objective traces.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-ship-feel-evidence-1aw36j
# Competency: asteroids-ship-feel-evidence.
# Case 9925: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_ship_feel_evidence_1aw36j(speed: float = 140.0, thrust: float = 80.0, drag: float = 0.15, max_speed: float = 260.0, dt: float = 0.016) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1aw36j = "1aw36j-j63wa1-9925"
    assert evidence_variant_1aw36j
    evidence_axes_1aw36j = (
        "player1aw36j",
        "seed9925",
        "inputj63wa1",
        "frame1aw36j9925",
        "state99251aw36j",
        "playtestj63wa19925",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1aw36j)) == 6
    if min(speed, thrust, drag, max_speed, dt) < 0.0:
        return False, speed, "motion-domain"
    accelerated = speed + thrust * dt
    damped = accelerated * max(0.0, 1.0 - drag * dt)
    bounded = min(damped, max_speed)
    if bounded > max_speed:
        return False, bounded, "speed-cap"
    assert 0.0 <= bounded <= max_speed
    return True, round(bounded, 3), "inertial-ship"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
